description: You are a forensic science technician analyzing glass fragments to determine their origin using the MASS::fgl dataset. Apply Compositional Data Analysis (CoDa) techniques to the chemical composition data (Refractive Index and 8 oxides). Calculate the geometric mean of each chemical oxide (columns Na through Fe) grouped by glass type and perform a Centered Log-Ratio (CLR) transformation on the 8 chemical columns. Generate a compositional biplot showing the separation of glass types and a ternary diagram showing the relative proportions of the three most abundant oxides (Si, Na, and Ca). Save the geometric means to /home/ga/RProjects/output/glass_geometric_means.csv, the CLR-transformed data to /home/ga/RProjects/output/glass_clr_transformed.csv, the biplot to /home/ga/RProjects/output/glass_biplot.png, and the ternary diagram to /home/ga/RProjects/output/glass_ternary_si_na_ca.png. Write the analysis code in /home/ga/RProjects/glass_analysis.R.
